cache_configuration.note: bloom_authoritative=false (the safe default) disables branch A — cache misses fall back to RocksDB RMD read. Branches B.1 (cache hit) and B.2.b (fallback) are active; B.2.a is unreachable because the high-watermark initializes to Long.MAX_VALUE to preserve DCR correctness when the partition may have pre-existing records from batch push.

e2e_throughput_ops_per_sec.notes: E2E = records produced / (iteration start -> sentinel keys visible on DC0 router).

baseline_e2e_throughput_ops_per_sec.note: Baseline is the locked value from rmd-cache-baseline.json. Master-agent verification criterion 4 requires >=15%, which this run does NOT meet; the improvement is nonetheless real and consistent across iterations (vs baseline iter-1/iter-2 spread of 84360..100612).

jmh_internal_throughput_ops_per_sec.notes: JMH's own throughput. Excludes sentinel drain in iteration teardown.

rmd_cache_metrics.notes: Aggregate ratio=0.999 over 2 measurement iterations. Criterion 3 requires >=0.85, which this run clearly exceeds.

baseline_comparison.note: Allocation rate per second is +9.6% vs baseline, driven by higher throughput. Per-op allocation is -5.7% vs baseline — strictly better memory profile per unit of work. Well within the criterion-7 +15% bound.
